data.title: Crypto Showdown: Fetch.ai and Ocean Protocol Clash Over $84M in Tokens
data.description:
Fetch. ai CEO Humayun Sheikh has publicly accused the Ocean Protocol Foundation of mismanaging 286 million FET tokens valued at about $84 million, a dispute that has sparked legal threats and drawn a response from cryptocurrency exchange Binance. Key points: The dispute traces back to the formation of the Artificial Superintelligence (ASI) Alliance in 2024, an initiative that unified three leading AI-driven blockchain projects, Fetch. ai, Ocean Protocol, and SingularityNET, under a single token structure aimed at advancing decentralized artificial intelligence collaboration
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/10/17/crypto-showdown-fetch-ai-and-ocean-protocol-clash-over-84m-in-tokens/

data.title: Bank of England Sets ‘Temporary’ Stablecoin Caps — With No End in Sight
data.description:
The Bank of England will impose temporary stablecoin caps, £10K–£20K for individuals and £10M for businesses, to protect credit, with no set timeline for lifting them. The post Bank of England Sets ‘Temporary’ Stablecoin Caps — With No End in Sight first appeared on The Shib Daily.
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/10/17/bank-of-england-sets-temporary-stablecoin-caps-with-no-end-in-sight/

data.title: Trump Confirms US Is in a Trade War With China — Bitcoin Feels the Sting
data.description:
President Donald Trump confirmed that the U. S. is now in an active trade war with China after threatening 100% tariffs on Chinese imports. The announcement, sparked by China’s tightened export controls on rare earth minerals, triggered a steep Bitcoin sell-off and renewed global market volatility
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/10/17/trump-confirms-us-is-in-a-trade-war-with-china-bitcoin-feels-the-sting/

data.title: France Hits Binance and Coinbase with AML Checks Ahead of EU License Decisions
data.description:
France is conducting anti-money laundering inspections on dozens of crypto exchanges including Binance and Coinhouse as regulators determine which of over 100 registered entities will receive EU-wide MiCA operating permits, with only Deblock, GOin, Bitstack, and CACEIS approved ahead of June 2026 deadline.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/france-hits-binance-and-coinbase-with-aml-checks-ahead-of-eu-license-decisions/

data.title: US credit card debt surges to new all-time high of $1.33 trillion
data.description:
The total debt Americans owe on their credit cards has just hit $1. 33T, a new record high that shows how deep household borrowing has gone in 2025.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-credit-card-debt-surges-to-all-time-high/

data.title: Sun Wukong adds CLO/USDT and YB/USDT, and the second phase of "Genesis Pioneer" is launched simultaneously
data.description: According to official news, the Chinese decentralized contract trading platform Sun Wukong has added CLO/USDT and YB/USDT, supporting up to 20x leverage. Simultaneously, the platform's "Genesis Pioneer Phase II" promotion has officially launched, with a comprehensive upgrade to its rebate policy: an additional 10% commission for the first month and a three-month exemption from assessments. Limited spots are available on a first-come, first-served basis.

data.title: Global safe-haven demand surges amid bad loan problems at two U.S. banks
data.description: The yen's continued strength pushed the USD/JPY pair below 150, sliding from an eight-month high, as soured loans at two US banks fueled global demand for safe-haven assets. The yen outperformed most of its G-10 peers on Friday, falling over 0.5% to around 149.63 at press time, its lowest level since October 6. The Swiss franc also rose, while both the dollar and US Treasury yields fell amid a sell-off in regional bank stocks. Strategist Mark Cranfield noted that forex traders looking back to 2023 will note that during that regional banking crisis, the USD/JPY exchange rate fell by approximately 800 pips from peak to trough. A similar pattern this time would mean the pair would fall towards its range low of 146 this month. The key driver was once again the plunge in US Treasury yields, with the 2-year Treasury yield now at levels last seen three years ago. With traders pricing in the Fed's target rate reaching the 3% region, there is still ample room for overshoot on the downside.
